a: Identifiers for media group types in the Aperture media source. These constants are used to specify a media group’s [typeIdentifier](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmediagroup/typeidentifier) attribute.

t: Final Cut Pro Media Group Type Identifiers
a: Identifiers for media group types in the Final Cut Pro media source. These constants are used to specify a media group’s [typeIdentifier](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmediagroup/typeidentifier) attribute.

Identifiers for media group types in folder-based media sources. These constants are used to specify a media group’s [typeIdentifier](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmediagroup/typeidentifier) attribute.

Identifiers for media group types in the GarageBand media source. These constants are used to specify a media group’s [typeIdentifier](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmediagroup/typeidentifier) attribute.
Keys used to specify the `options` dictionary argument to [init(options:)](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialibrary/init(options:)).
Identifiers for media group types in the Logic media source. These constants are used to specify a media group’s [typeIdentifier](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmediagroup/typeidentifier) attribute.
The media group that represents all photos in Aperture.
The media group that represents all projects in Aperture.
A media group that represents a Facebook album that is visible in Aperture.
A media group that represents a Facebook user account in Aperture. A Facebook user account contains one or more Facebook albums.
A media group that represents a Faces album in Aperture. Individual Faces albums are nested in the main Faces album.
The media group that represents the album of flagged media in Aperture.
A media group that represents a Flickr album that is visible in Aperture.
A media group that represents a Flickr user account in Aperture. A Flickr user account contains one or more Flickr albums.
A media group that represents a folder in Aperture.
The media group that represents the last import album in Aperture.
The media group that represents the recent content album in Aperture, known as the Last *N* Months album. The value for *N* is usually 12 (settable in Aperture > Preferences > General).
The media group that represents the last viewed event in Aperture.
A media group that represents a light table in Aperture.
A media group that represents a photo stream in Aperture.
The media group that represents the Places album in Aperture.
A media group that represents a Places album for a city in Aperture. A city album is nested in a province or state album.
A media group that represents a Places album for a country or region in Aperture. A country or region album is nested in the main Places album.
A media group that represents a Places album for a point-of-interest in Aperture. A point of interest album is nested in a city album.
A media group that represents a Places album for a province or state in Aperture. A province or state album is nested in a country or region album.
A media group that represents a project in Aperture.
A media group that represents a folder within a project in Aperture.
The root media group for Aperture.
The media group that represents a slideshow in Aperture.
A media group that represents a SmugMug album that is visible in Aperture.
A media group that represents a SmugMug user account in Aperture. A SmugMug user account contains one or more SmugMug albums.
A media group that represents a user-created album in Aperture.
A media group that represents a user-created smart album in Aperture.
A media group that represents a collection of events from a specific time period in Final Cut Pro.
A media group that represents an event in Final Cut Pro.
The media group that represents the event library in Final Cut Pro. The event library contains all event calendar groups.
A media group that represents a folder in Final Cut Pro.
A media group that represents a project in Final Cut Pro.
The root media group for Final Cut Pro.
A media group that represents a folder in folder-based media.
The root media group for folder-based media.
A media group that represents a folder in GarageBand.
The root media group for GarageBand.
The media group that represents all bounces in Logic.
A media group that represents a project in Logic. Projects may be nested.
The media group that represents all projects in Logic.
The root media group for Logic.

The `MLMediaGroup` class provides groupings for media objects from a single source of media, such as iTunes or Aperture. The media objects—individual files containing a piece of media such as a photo, song, or movie—are referenced by one or more groups within each media source. These groupings serve as filters, providing hierarchical structure to the collection of objects in each source.
The `MLMediaLibrary` class provides an interface for accessing a collection of media objects from various sources. It serves as the initial access point of the Media Library framework.
Specifies one or more relative paths inside the caller’s app bundle in which to search for media files. The value for this key is an array of strings (relative paths inside the caller’s app bundle).
Identifies the folder containing audio loops from Apple.

Defines which media sources to exclude when loading. This option is processed after [MLMediaLoadIncludeSourcesKey](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadincludesourceskey). The value for this key is an array of strings (media source identifiers). For a list of valid media source identifiers, see [Media Source Identifiers](/documentation/medialibrary/media-source-identifiers).

Specifies the well-known folders that should be searched for media files. If this key is not present, none of the well-known folders will be provided. The value for this key is an array of strings (identifiers that correspond to well-known folder locations). For a list of well-known folder identifiers, see [Well-Known Folder Identifiers](/documentation/medialibrary/well-known-folder-identifiers).

Defines which media sources to include when loading. If not present, load all available media sources. This option is processed after [MLMediaLoadSourceTypesKey](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadsourcetypeskey). If [MLMediaLoadIncludeSourcesKey](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadincludesourceskey) is present but [MLMediaLoadSourceTypesKey](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadsourcetypeskey) is not, then only those sources specified here will be loaded. This is useful for loading a single media source. When both keys are present, this is useful for adding one or more media sources that normally would not appear for the requested library type. The value for this key is an array of strings (media source identifiers). For a list of valid media source identifiers, see [Media Source Identifiers](/documentation/medialibrary/media-source-identifiers).
Identifies the user’s Movies folder.

Defines which sources to load based on library type. If not present, this will load all sources. The value for this key is a media source type. For a list of valid media source types, see [MLMediaSourceType](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmediasourcetype).

The `MLMediaObject` class describes a single media file, such as a photo, song, or movie. Each media object contains basic metadata including a name, media type, URL, and so on. Additional information about each object is stored in its list of attributes. For a list of possible object attribute keys, see [Media Object Attribute Keys](/documentation/medialibrary/media-object-attribute-keys).
Specifies the media object’s album. The value for this key is a string ([NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString)).
Specifies the media object’s artist. The value for this key is a string ([NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString)).
Specifies the media object’s bit rate, in kilobits per second. The value for this key is a number ([NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber)).
Specifies the media object’s channel count. The value for this key is a number ([NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber)).
Specifies the contents of the comments field associated with the media object. The value for this key is a string ([NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString)).
Specifies the media object’s duration, in seconds. The value for this key is a number ([NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber)).
Specifies the media object’s genre. The value for this key is a string ([NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString)).
Specifies the keywords associated with the media object. The value for this key is an array ([NSArray](/documentation/Foundation/NSArray)) of strings ([NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString)).
Used by iTunes only. Specifies the media object’s file format (shown in the “Kind” column in iTunes). The value for this key is a string ([NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString)).

Specifies whether the media object is protected by DRM (Digital Rights Management). The value for this key is a number ([NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber)), 0 or 1, that represents a Boolean value.

Specifies the media object’s resolution. The value for this key is a string ([NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString)) intended to be converted to a size ([NSSize](/documentation/Foundation/NSSize)) using the [NSSizeFromString(_:)](/documentation/Foundation/NSSizeFromString(_:)) method.
Specifies the media object’s sample rate, in samples per second (Hz). The value for this key is a number ([NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber)).
Specifies the media object’s track number. The value for this key is a number ([NSNumber](/documentation/Foundation/NSNumber)).

The `MLMediaSource` class identifies a specific provider of media. Conceptually, a media source respresents a single app, such as iTunes or Aperture. Each media source contains multiple groups of media objects—individual files containing a piece of media such as a photo, song, or movie.
The media source providing content from Aperture.

The media source for app-defined folders. This identifies a media source created from a relative path inside the caller’s app bundle. This source provides data when [MLMediaLoadAppFoldersKey](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadappfolderskey) is provided in the options.

The media source for custom folders. Currently, the only custom folder is the folder containing audio loops from Apple. This source provides data when [MLMediaLoadFoldersKey](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadfolderskey) is provided with the [MLMediaLoadAppleLoops](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadappleloops) value.
The media source providing content from Final Cut Pro.
The media source providing content from GarageBand.
The media source providing content from Logic.

The media source for the user’s Movies folder. This source provides data when [MLMediaLoadFoldersKey](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadfolderskey) is provided with the [MLMediaLoadMoviesFolder](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadmoviesfolder) value.
The media source providing content from Photo Booth.

Specifies the source type associated with a particular media source. Source type reflects the primary type of media within the source. These constants are used to specify values for [MLMediaLoadSourceTypesKey](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialoadsourcetypeskey) in the [init(options:)](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialibrary/init(options:)) method of [MLMediaLibrary](/documentation/medialibrary/mlmedialibrary).
The media source providing content from iMovie.
The media source providing content from iPhoto.
The media source providing content from iTunes.
